page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor running at a clock speed of 2.55 GHz. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a strong single-core CPU capability, with stress-ng single-core tests placing it in the top 25% of its class. However, multi-core performance is comparatively limited, scoring in the bottom 25% due to the dual-core configuration. Memory bandwidth is a notable bottleneck, with read-only cached small block operations performing in the bottom 10% and uncached large block transfers in the bottom 25%. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance, while complex multi-threaded workloads, such as software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, score in the lower tiers.

Given its dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource ratio, the ecs.g7a.large offers a stable entry-level platform for workloads that require predictable single-core performance without the overhead of shared-core burstable models. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for lightweight applications that do not demand high memory bandwidth or local storage IOPS. This instance is best suited for basic web serving, single-threaded background processing, lightweight text processing, and small-scale development or testing environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose virtual server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7T83 processor with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmark data reveals strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core capabilities and memory bandwidth are relatively constrained. With a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated CPU resources without high multi-threaded demands. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded background tasks, and basic development environments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose instance featuring 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 1 Gbps network bandwidth, offering strong single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g7a.large is a general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and KVM-based virtualization.
tagline: A dedicated dual-core AMD EPYC instance offering balanced memory and strong single-core performance for entry-level workloads.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated AMD EPYC 7T83 vCPUs at 2.55 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory with no local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance in benchmark testing
Suited for lightweight web serving and single-threaded tasks
